Skip to content

This site is powered by Granicus.

To provide you with the best online experience we use a range of essential site cookies. These essential cookies enable you to remain logged in and for your site preferences to be remembered, in addition to other essential functions. Granicus does not use analytics, advertising or tracking cookies. In addition to these essential cookies, the organisation which owns this site may choose to place supplementary cookies, for example to support analytics. Should they choose to do this, they have an obligation to let you know where this is the case, and what the benefits and costs are, who they share it with, and what options you have.

response_body.Name: DD_Guarantee
response_body.Value:
The Direct Debit Guarantee


• This Guarantee is offered by all Banks and Building Societies that accept instructions to pay Direct Debits.


• If there are any changes to the amount, date or frequency of your Direct Debit we will notify you 10 working days in advance of your account being debited or as otherwise agreed. If you request us to collect a payment, confirmation of the amount and date will be given to you at the time of the request.


• If an error is made in the payment of your Direct Debit by us or your bank or building society, you are entitled to a full and immediate refund of the amount paid from your bank or building society.


• If you receive a refund you are not entitled to, you must pay it back when we ask you to.


• You can cancel a Direct Debit at any time by simply contacting your bank or building society. Written confirmation may be required. Please also notify us.

DP_Para_RBE
response_body.Value:
Privacy notice

Portsmouth City Council will process your personal information appropriately and legally in accordance with the relevant national legislation. The details you provide will be held in a secure system or database and will only be shared with other organisations where the law allows.

We may be required by law to disclose your personal information without your consent for the purposes of protecting the council's legitimate interests (for example to recover debts that are owed to Portsmouth City Council), to fulfil a legal obligation (such as bringing empty properties back into use) or for the purposes of preventing or detecting crime/fraud or apprehending and prosecuting offenders (for example to the Police, the Cabinet Office or Department for Work and Pension or as part of the National Fraud Initiative) or where we have a statutory duty to do so. For more information visit the National Fraud Initiative.

Portsmouth City Council uses an online form service supplied by Granicus.

Granicus does not use your personal information for anything other than providing the service to the Council and will not pass on your information for promotional purposes to any other person or organisation (third parties).

For more information about how Granicus uses personal information, view the Granicus govService Subscriber Privacy Policy
For more information about how Portsmouth City Council uses personal information, view the Council's Data Protection Privacy Notice

response_body.Name: DP_Para_FC
response_body.Value:
Privacy notice

Portsmouth City Council will process your personal information in accordance with data protection law. The personal details provided by you will be used by PCC Fostering Service for the purposes of caring for looked after children. Your details will be held on a Firmstep database and where the law allows, may be shared with other departments within the council for the purposes of updating details they hold about you. The council may also be required to disclose personal information to third parties such as NHS for the purposes of sharing healthcare data or Police, Department for Work and Pensions or for the National Fraud Initiative for the purposes of detecting crime or apprehending or prosecuting offenders.

For further information about how the Council collects and uses personal information please visit our website: https://www.portsmouth.gov.uk/ext/the-council/data-protection-privacy-notice

response_body.Name: RRM_emailBody_redHanger
response_body.Value:
The contractor has fed back to us that your recycling bin was contaminated, this means that there is something in the bin that we do not take. To clarify, we only take the following items as recycling:

  • Paper (not shredded)
  • Card
  • Plastic bottles (no other plastics)
  • Cans
  • Tins
  • Aerosols

    • Please check your bin and remove any items not listed above; because it was reported as contaminated, the contractor will not be returning to collect it so you will need to keep it until the next collection. If you have surplus recycling next time, you can put it in a cardboard box beside your recycling bin.

response_body.Name: ExitInterview
response_body.Value:
Exit Interviews

Employees who are leaving PCC voluntarily are entitled to an exit interview. Please follow the link to the Exit Interview policy.

Please read the exit interview policy and undertake the exit interview as appropriate. This can be a fairly informal process and should be recorded on the exit interview form. At the start of the exit interview, you should explain the purpose of the interview and explain that this will not affect their work record or any future references.

You also need to explain that a record of the interview will be kept and also shared with appropriate parties. You can agree that this does not happen until after they have left. Completed forms need to be returned to HR as soon as it is completed or within one week of the individual leaving.

If the individual declines an exit interview, or if for operational reasons you are unable to undertake an exit interview with the individual, then please encourage them to complete the Exit Questionnaire on EBS under HR Employee Self-Service.

response_body.Name: UniversalCreditStatment
response_body.Value:
You can no longer make a new claim for Housing Benefit (HB) in this area unless you are in one of the groups that can still make a claim. If you cannot claim Housing Benefit you will need to claim Universal Credit (UC).

If you can answer yes to any of the following questions please continue with your claim for housing benefit

  • Do you live in supported accommodation and receive care support or supervision from your landlord or by an organisation on your landlord’s behalf?
  • Have you been placed in temporary accommodation by the Housing Team as part of their duties under homelessness legislation?
  • Are you a single claimant that has reached state pension age?
  • Are you part of a couple who have both reached state pension age?
  • Are you a member of a couple where one member has reached state pension age and
    • One member is under 18, or
    • One member is absent from home due to being in prison for 6 months or longer, or
    • One member is not eligible to claim benefits in the UK, or
    • One of you is absent from Great Britain for more than one month

If you claim UC your rent will be paid by the Department for Work and Pensions (DWP) but you will need to make a claim for Council Tax Reduction (CTR) with us, please continue with your claim for council tax support only.

Portsmouth City Council's Street Naming & Numbering service will use your details to communicate with you for the purpose of street naming & numbering only. We will retain this information for the following periods:

  • Application for new street name, alterations to existing street name - 7 years after last action.
  • Application for property, alterations to existing property or corrections to existing property - 7 years after last action.
  • Official decision/notification document approving a new street name or approving a rename to an existing street - Permanent
  • Official decision/notification document approving a new property number or name and approving a renumber or rename to an existing property - Permanent

response_body.Value:
Privacy notice

Portsmouth City Council will process your personal information appropriately and legally in accordance with the relevant national legislation. The personal details provided by you will be used by Private Sector Housing for the purposes of mandatory HMO licensing. The details you provide will be held in a secure system or database and will only be shared with other organisations where the law allows.

Your information may be shared with other Portsmouth City Council departments, such as the Planning department, for the purposes of keeping an HMO database / updating details they hold about you. The council may also be required to disclose personal information to third parties, such as other local authorities issuing HMO licenses or Police, Department for Work and Pensions or for the National Fraud Initiative for the purposes of preventing or detecting crime or apprehending or prosecuting offenders. Some of this data, including the license holder's name, address and contact details, is required by law to be displayed on a public register.

Portsmouth City Council uses a secure online form service supplied by Granicus.

response_body.Name: ElectricVehicleSubmission
response_body.Value:
Thank you for your interest in the On-Street Residential Charge Point Scheme (ORCS) for Portsmouth. Your {form_name} form has been received.

The reference number is {case_ref}.

We shall review your request and study the technical feasibility of the proposed location. If affirmative, we will then add it to the bid that we will submit to the Office for Zero Emission Vehicles (OZEV) for funding for the on-street charge points.

Please note that council-installed on-street charging points/bays are for the use of any electric vehicle driver and not just the original requestor of the facility.

You can find details of all existing charge points in Portsmouth via: https://travel.portsmouth.gov.uk/electric-vehicle-charge-point-locations/

The installation date is yet to be determined, but you can access the following link for updates about the scheme - EV charging

We hope this information is helpful. We have an online Frequently Asked Questions list but do contact us at transportplan@portsmouthcc.gov.uk with any further queries.

response_body.Name: protocol_mayor
response_body.Value:
The following notes will help you through the protocol which can be involved in a visit by the Lord Mayor, but please do not hesitate to phone the Lord Mayor's Office should you need any further advice on (023) 9283 4706.

It will help others involved in the visit if you pass this information on to them.

MEET AND GREET

  • Please ensure that the Lord Mayor (and Lady Mayoress if attending) are met on arrival and escorted throughout the engagement. The person escorting them should also explain precisely what duties the Lord Mayor and/or Lady Mayoress are expected to perform.

FORMS OF ADDRESS

  • In writing: 'The Lord Mayor of Portsmouth, Councillor Jason Fazackarley'
  • In writing, including the Lady Mayoress 'The Lord Mayor of Portsmouth and the Lady Mayoress, Councillor Jason Fazackarley and Miss Helen Mitchell'
  • In speech: either 'My Lord Mayor' (usually at the beginning of a formal speech) or 'Lord Mayor' (in everyday speech)
  • In speech for the Lady Mayoress 'Lady Mayoress'
  • On place cards: 'The Lord Mayor of Portsmouth' 'The Lady Mayoress of Portsmouth'
  • For inclusion on a plaque etc 'The Right Worshipful the Lord Mayor of Portsmouth'

SEATING PLANS

  • The Lord Mayor should be seated on the immediate right hand side of the host (ess), with the Lady Mayoress on the host (ess) immediate left.
  • If there are other distinguished guests attending, please consult the Lord Mayor's Office as to the most appropriate seating arrangement.

env_tokens.DD_Guarantee:
The Direct Debit Guarantee


• This Guarantee is offered by all Banks and Building Societies that accept instructions to pay Direct Debits.


• If there are any changes to the amount, date or frequency of your Direct Debit we will notify you 10 working days in advance of your account being debited or as otherwise agreed. If you request us to collect a payment, confirmation of the amount and date will be given to you at the time of the request.


• If an error is made in the payment of your Direct Debit by us or your bank or building society, you are entitled to a full and immediate refund of the amount paid from your bank or building society.


• If you receive a refund you are not entitled to, you must pay it back when we ask you to.


• You can cancel a Direct Debit at any time by simply contacting your bank or building society. Written confirmation may be required. Please also notify us.

env_tokens.RRM_emailBody_redHanger:
The contractor has fed back to us that your recycling bin was contaminated, this means that there is something in the bin that we do not take. To clarify, we only take the following items as recycling:

  • Paper (not shredded)
  • Card
  • Plastic bottles (no other plastics)
  • Cans
  • Tins
  • Aerosols

    • Please check your bin and remove any items not listed above; because it was reported as contaminated, the contractor will not be returning to collect it so you will need to keep it until the next collection. If you have surplus recycling next time, you can put it in a cardboard box beside your recycling bin.
env_tokens.ExitInterview:
Exit Interviews

Employees who are leaving PCC voluntarily are entitled to an exit interview. Please follow the link to the Exit Interview policy.

Please read the exit interview policy and undertake the exit interview as appropriate. This can be a fairly informal process and should be recorded on the exit interview form. At the start of the exit interview, you should explain the purpose of the interview and explain that this will not affect their work record or any future references.

You also need to explain that a record of the interview will be kept and also shared with appropriate parties. You can agree that this does not happen until after they have left. Completed forms need to be returned to HR as soon as it is completed or within one week of the individual leaving.

If the individual declines an exit interview, or if for operational reasons you are unable to undertake an exit interview with the individual, then please encourage them to complete the Exit Questionnaire on EBS under HR Employee Self-Service.
env_tokens.UniversalCreditStatment:
You can no longer make a new claim for Housing Benefit (HB) in this area unless you are in one of the groups that can still make a claim. If you cannot claim Housing Benefit you will need to claim Universal Credit (UC).

If you can answer yes to any of the following questions please continue with your claim for housing benefit

  • Do you live in supported accommodation and receive care support or supervision from your landlord or by an organisation on your landlord’s behalf?
  • Have you been placed in temporary accommodation by the Housing Team as part of their duties under homelessness legislation?
  • Are you a single claimant that has reached state pension age?
  • Are you part of a couple who have both reached state pension age?
  • Are you a member of a couple where one member has reached state pension age and
    • One member is under 18, or
    • One member is absent from home due to being in prison for 6 months or longer, or
    • One member is not eligible to claim benefits in the UK, or
    • One of you is absent from Great Britain for more than one month

If you claim UC your rent will be paid by the Department for Work and Pensions (DWP) but you will need to make a claim for Council Tax Reduction (CTR) with us, please continue with your claim for council tax support only.

Thank you for your interest in the On-Street Residential Charge Point Scheme (ORCS) for Portsmouth. Your {form_name} form has been received.

The reference number is {case_ref}.

We shall review your request and study the technical feasibility of the proposed location. If affirmative, we will then add it to the bid that we will submit to the Office for Zero Emission Vehicles (OZEV) for funding for the on-street charge points.

Please note that council-installed on-street charging points/bays are for the use of any electric vehicle driver and not just the original requestor of the facility.

You can find details of all existing charge points in Portsmouth via: https://travel.portsmouth.gov.uk/electric-vehicle-charge-point-locations/

The installation date is yet to be determined, but you can access the following link for updates about the scheme - EV charging

We hope this information is helpful. We have an online Frequently Asked Questions list but do contact us at transportplan@portsmouthcc.gov.uk with any further queries.

env_tokens.protocol_mayor:
The following notes will help you through the protocol which can be involved in a visit by the Lord Mayor, but please do not hesitate to phone the Lord Mayor's Office should you need any further advice on (023) 9283 4706.

It will help others involved in the visit if you pass this information on to them.

MEET AND GREET

  • Please ensure that the Lord Mayor (and Lady Mayoress if attending) are met on arrival and escorted throughout the engagement. The person escorting them should also explain precisely what duties the Lord Mayor and/or Lady Mayoress are expected to perform.

FORMS OF ADDRESS

  • In writing: 'The Lord Mayor of Portsmouth, Councillor Jason Fazackarley'
  • In writing, including the Lady Mayoress 'The Lord Mayor of Portsmouth and the Lady Mayoress, Councillor Jason Fazackarley and Miss Helen Mitchell'
  • In speech: either 'My Lord Mayor' (usually at the beginning of a formal speech) or 'Lord Mayor' (in everyday speech)
  • In speech for the Lady Mayoress 'Lady Mayoress'
  • On place cards: 'The Lord Mayor of Portsmouth' 'The Lady Mayoress of Portsmouth'
  • For inclusion on a plaque etc 'The Right Worshipful the Lord Mayor of Portsmouth'

SEATING PLANS

  • The Lord Mayor should be seated on the immediate right hand side of the host (ess), with the Lady Mayoress on the host (ess) immediate left.
  • If there are other distinguished guests attending, please consult the Lord Mayor's Office as to the most appropriate seating arrangement.

env_tokens.DD_Guarantee:
The Direct Debit Guarantee


• This Guarantee is offered by all Banks and Building Societies that accept instructions to pay Direct Debits.


• If there are any changes to the amount, date or frequency of your Direct Debit we will notify you 10 working days in advance of your account being debited or as otherwise agreed. If you request us to collect a payment, confirmation of the amount and date will be given to you at the time of the request.


• If an error is made in the payment of your Direct Debit by us or your bank or building society, you are entitled to a full and immediate refund of the amount paid from your bank or building society.


• If you receive a refund you are not entitled to, you must pay it back when we ask you to.


• You can cancel a Direct Debit at any time by simply contacting your bank or building society. Written confirmation may be required. Please also notify us.

env_tokens.RRM_emailBody_redHanger:
The contractor has fed back to us that your recycling bin was contaminated, this means that there is something in the bin that we do not take. To clarify, we only take the following items as recycling:

  • Paper (not shredded)
  • Card
  • Plastic bottles (no other plastics)
  • Cans
  • Tins
  • Aerosols

    • Please check your bin and remove any items not listed above; because it was reported as contaminated, the contractor will not be returning to collect it so you will need to keep it until the next collection. If you have surplus recycling next time, you can put it in a cardboard box beside your recycling bin.
env_tokens.ExitInterview:
Exit Interviews

Employees who are leaving PCC voluntarily are entitled to an exit interview. Please follow the link to the Exit Interview policy.

Please read the exit interview policy and undertake the exit interview as appropriate. This can be a fairly informal process and should be recorded on the exit interview form. At the start of the exit interview, you should explain the purpose of the interview and explain that this will not affect their work record or any future references.

You also need to explain that a record of the interview will be kept and also shared with appropriate parties. You can agree that this does not happen until after they have left. Completed forms need to be returned to HR as soon as it is completed or within one week of the individual leaving.

If the individual declines an exit interview, or if for operational reasons you are unable to undertake an exit interview with the individual, then please encourage them to complete the Exit Questionnaire on EBS under HR Employee Self-Service.
env_tokens.UniversalCreditStatment:
You can no longer make a new claim for Housing Benefit (HB) in this area unless you are in one of the groups that can still make a claim. If you cannot claim Housing Benefit you will need to claim Universal Credit (UC).

If you can answer yes to any of the following questions please continue with your claim for housing benefit

  • Do you live in supported accommodation and receive care support or supervision from your landlord or by an organisation on your landlord’s behalf?
  • Have you been placed in temporary accommodation by the Housing Team as part of their duties under homelessness legislation?
  • Are you a single claimant that has reached state pension age?
  • Are you part of a couple who have both reached state pension age?
  • Are you a member of a couple where one member has reached state pension age and
    • One member is under 18, or
    • One member is absent from home due to being in prison for 6 months or longer, or
    • One member is not eligible to claim benefits in the UK, or
    • One of you is absent from Great Britain for more than one month

If you claim UC your rent will be paid by the Department for Work and Pensions (DWP) but you will need to make a claim for Council Tax Reduction (CTR) with us, please continue with your claim for council tax support only.

env_tokens.protocol_mayor:
The following notes will help you through the protocol which can be involved in a visit by the Lord Mayor, but please do not hesitate to phone the Lord Mayor's Office should you need any further advice on (023) 9283 4706.

It will help others involved in the visit if you pass this information on to them.

MEET AND GREET

  • Please ensure that the Lord Mayor (and Lady Mayoress if attending) are met on arrival and escorted throughout the engagement. The person escorting them should also explain precisely what duties the Lord Mayor and/or Lady Mayoress are expected to perform.

FORMS OF ADDRESS

  • In writing: 'The Lord Mayor of Portsmouth, Councillor Jason Fazackarley'
  • In writing, including the Lady Mayoress 'The Lord Mayor of Portsmouth and the Lady Mayoress, Councillor Jason Fazackarley and Miss Helen Mitchell'
  • In speech: either 'My Lord Mayor' (usually at the beginning of a formal speech) or 'Lord Mayor' (in everyday speech)
  • In speech for the Lady Mayoress 'Lady Mayoress'
  • On place cards: 'The Lord Mayor of Portsmouth' 'The Lady Mayoress of Portsmouth'
  • For inclusion on a plaque etc 'The Right Worshipful the Lord Mayor of Portsmouth'

SEATING PLANS

  • The Lord Mayor should be seated on the immediate right hand side of the host (ess), with the Lady Mayoress on the host (ess) immediate left.
  • If there are other distinguished guests attending, please consult the Lord Mayor's Office as to the most appropriate seating arrangement.

env_tokens.DD_Guarantee:
The Direct Debit Guarantee


• This Guarantee is offered by all Banks and Building Societies that accept instructions to pay Direct Debits.


• If there are any changes to the amount, date or frequency of your Direct Debit we will notify you 10 working days in advance of your account being debited or as otherwise agreed. If you request us to collect a payment, confirmation of the amount and date will be given to you at the time of the request.


• If an error is made in the payment of your Direct Debit by us or your bank or building society, you are entitled to a full and immediate refund of the amount paid from your bank or building society.


• If you receive a refund you are not entitled to, you must pay it back when we ask you to.


• You can cancel a Direct Debit at any time by simply contacting your bank or building society. Written confirmation may be required. Please also notify us.

env_tokens.RRM_emailBody_redHanger:
The contractor has fed back to us that your recycling bin was contaminated, this means that there is something in the bin that we do not take. To clarify, we only take the following items as recycling:

  • Paper (not shredded)
  • Card
  • Plastic bottles (no other plastics)
  • Cans
  • Tins
  • Aerosols

    • Please check your bin and remove any items not listed above; because it was reported as contaminated, the contractor will not be returning to collect it so you will need to keep it until the next collection. If you have surplus recycling next time, you can put it in a cardboard box beside your recycling bin.
env_tokens.ExitInterview:
Exit Interviews

Employees who are leaving PCC voluntarily are entitled to an exit interview. Please follow the link to the Exit Interview policy.

Please read the exit interview policy and undertake the exit interview as appropriate. This can be a fairly informal process and should be recorded on the exit interview form. At the start of the exit interview, you should explain the purpose of the interview and explain that this will not affect their work record or any future references.

You also need to explain that a record of the interview will be kept and also shared with appropriate parties. You can agree that this does not happen until after they have left. Completed forms need to be returned to HR as soon as it is completed or within one week of the individual leaving.

If the individual declines an exit interview, or if for operational reasons you are unable to undertake an exit interview with the individual, then please encourage them to complete the Exit Questionnaire on EBS under HR Employee Self-Service.
env_tokens.UniversalCreditStatment:
You can no longer make a new claim for Housing Benefit (HB) in this area unless you are in one of the groups that can still make a claim. If you cannot claim Housing Benefit you will need to claim Universal Credit (UC).

If you can answer yes to any of the following questions please continue with your claim for housing benefit

  • Do you live in supported accommodation and receive care support or supervision from your landlord or by an organisation on your landlord’s behalf?
  • Have you been placed in temporary accommodation by the Housing Team as part of their duties under homelessness legislation?
  • Are you a single claimant that has reached state pension age?
  • Are you part of a couple who have both reached state pension age?
  • Are you a member of a couple where one member has reached state pension age and
    • One member is under 18, or
    • One member is absent from home due to being in prison for 6 months or longer, or
    • One member is not eligible to claim benefits in the UK, or
    • One of you is absent from Great Britain for more than one month

If you claim UC your rent will be paid by the Department for Work and Pensions (DWP) but you will need to make a claim for Council Tax Reduction (CTR) with us, please continue with your claim for council tax support only.

env_tokens.ElectricVehicleSubmission:
Thank you for your interest in the On-Street Residential Charge Point Scheme (ORCS) for Portsmouth. Your {form_name} form has been received.

The reference number is {case_ref}.

We shall review your request and study the technical feasibility of the proposed location. If affirmative, we will then add it to the bid that we will submit to the Office for Zero Emission Vehicles (OZEV) for funding for the on-street charge points.

Please note that council-installed on-street charging points/bays are for the use of any electric vehicle driver and not just the original requestor of the facility.

You can find details of all existing charge points in Portsmouth via: https://travel.portsmouth.gov.uk/electric-vehicle-charge-point-locations/

The installation date is yet to be determined, but you can access the following link for updates about the scheme - EV charging

We hope this information is helpful. We have an online Frequently Asked Questions list but do contact us at transportplan@portsmouthcc.gov.uk with any further queries.

env_tokens.GDPR_elections:
Privacy notice

The Electoral Registration Officer for Portsmouth City Council will process your personal information appropriately and legally in accordance with the relevant national legislation. The details you provide will be held in a secure system or database and will only be shared with other organisations where the law allows. Portsmouth City Council uses an online form service supplied by Granicus.

Granicus does not use your personal information for anything other than providing the service to the Council and will not pass on your information for promotional purposes to any other person or organisation (third parties).

For more information about how Granicus uses personal information, view the Granicus govService Subscriber Privacy Policy

For more information about how Portsmouth City Council uses personal information, view the Council's Electoral services privacy notice - Portsmouth City Council

env_tokens.DD_Guarantee:
The Direct Debit Guarantee


• This Guarantee is offered by all Banks and Building Societies that accept instructions to pay Direct Debits.


• If there are any changes to the amount, date or frequency of your Direct Debit we will notify you 10 working days in advance of your account being debited or as otherwise agreed. If you request us to collect a payment, confirmation of the amount and date will be given to you at the time of the request.


• If an error is made in the payment of your Direct Debit by us or your bank or building society, you are entitled to a full and immediate refund of the amount paid from your bank or building society.


• If you receive a refund you are not entitled to, you must pay it back when we ask you to.


• You can cancel a Direct Debit at any time by simply contacting your bank or building society. Written confirmation may be required. Please also notify us.

env_tokens.DP_Para_RBE:
Privacy notice

Portsmouth City Council will process your personal information appropriately and legally in accordance with the relevant national legislation. The details you provide will be held in a secure system or database and will only be shared with other organisations where the law allows.

We may be required by law to disclose your personal information without your consent for the purposes of protecting the council's legitimate interests (for example to recover debts that are owed to Portsmouth City Council), to fulfil a legal obligation (such as bringing empty properties back into use) or for the purposes of preventing or detecting crime/fraud or apprehending and prosecuting offenders (for example to the Police, the Cabinet Office or Department for Work and Pension or as part of the National Fraud Initiative) or where we have a statutory duty to do so. For more information visit the National Fraud Initiative.

Portsmouth City Council uses an online form service supplied by Granicus.

Granicus does not use your personal information for anything other than providing the service to the Council and will not pass on your information for promotional purposes to any other person or organisation (third parties).

For more information about how Granicus uses personal information, view the Granicus govService Subscriber Privacy Policy
For more information about how Portsmouth City Council uses personal information, view the Council's Data Protection Privacy Notice
env_tokens.DP_Para_FC:
Privacy notice

Portsmouth City Council will process your personal information in accordance with data protection law. The personal details provided by you will be used by PCC Fostering Service for the purposes of caring for looked after children. Your details will be held on a Firmstep database and where the law allows, may be shared with other departments within the council for the purposes of updating details they hold about you. The council may also be required to disclose personal information to third parties such as NHS for the purposes of sharing healthcare data or Police, Department for Work and Pensions or for the National Fraud Initiative for the purposes of detecting crime or apprehending or prosecuting offenders.

For further information about how the Council collects and uses personal information please visit our website: https://www.portsmouth.gov.uk/ext/the-council/data-protection-privacy-notice
env_tokens.DemSurvey:

env_tokens.RRM_emailBody_redHanger:
The contractor has fed back to us that your recycling bin was contaminated, this means that there is something in the bin that we do not take. To clarify, we only take the following items as recycling:

  • Paper (not shredded)
  • Card
  • Plastic bottles (no other plastics)
  • Cans
  • Tins
  • Aerosols

    • Please check your bin and remove any items not listed above; because it was reported as contaminated, the contractor will not be returning to collect it so you will need to keep it until the next collection. If you have surplus recycling next time, you can put it in a cardboard box beside your recycling bin.
env_tokens.ExitInterview:
Exit Interviews

Employees who are leaving PCC voluntarily are entitled to an exit interview. Please follow the link to the Exit Interview policy.

Please read the exit interview policy and undertake the exit interview as appropriate. This can be a fairly informal process and should be recorded on the exit interview form. At the start of the exit interview, you should explain the purpose of the interview and explain that this will not affect their work record or any future references.

You also need to explain that a record of the interview will be kept and also shared with appropriate parties. You can agree that this does not happen until after they have left. Completed forms need to be returned to HR as soon as it is completed or within one week of the individual leaving.

If the individual declines an exit interview, or if for operational reasons you are unable to undertake an exit interview with the individual, then please encourage them to complete the Exit Questionnaire on EBS under HR Employee Self-Service.
env_tokens.UniversalCreditStatment:
You can no longer make a new claim for Housing Benefit (HB) in this area unless you are in one of the groups that can still make a claim. If you cannot claim Housing Benefit you will need to claim Universal Credit (UC).

If you can answer yes to any of the following questions please continue with your claim for housing benefit

  • Do you live in supported accommodation and receive care support or supervision from your landlord or by an organisation on your landlord’s behalf?
  • Have you been placed in temporary accommodation by the Housing Team as part of their duties under homelessness legislation?
  • Are you a single claimant that has reached state pension age?
  • Are you part of a couple who have both reached state pension age?
  • Are you a member of a couple where one member has reached state pension age and
    • One member is under 18, or
    • One member is absent from home due to being in prison for 6 months or longer, or
    • One member is not eligible to claim benefits in the UK, or
    • One of you is absent from Great Britain for more than one month

If you claim UC your rent will be paid by the Department for Work and Pensions (DWP) but you will need to make a claim for Council Tax Reduction (CTR) with us, please continue with your claim for council tax support only.

  • Application for property, alterations to existing property or corrections to existing property - 7 years after last action.
  • Official decision/notification document approving a new street name or approving a rename to an existing street - Permanent
  • Official decision/notification document approving a new property number or name and approving a renumber or rename to an existing property - Permanent

env_tokens.ElectricVehicleSubmission:
Thank you for your interest in the On-Street Residential Charge Point Scheme (ORCS) for Portsmouth. Your {form_name} form has been received.

The reference number is {case_ref}.

We shall review your request and study the technical feasibility of the proposed location. If affirmative, we will then add it to the bid that we will submit to the Office for Zero Emission Vehicles (OZEV) for funding for the on-street charge points.

Please note that council-installed on-street charging points/bays are for the use of any electric vehicle driver and not just the original requestor of the facility.

You can find details of all existing charge points in Portsmouth via: https://travel.portsmouth.gov.uk/electric-vehicle-charge-point-locations/

The installation date is yet to be determined, but you can access the following link for updates about the scheme - EV charging

We hope this information is helpful. We have an online Frequently Asked Questions list but do contact us at transportplan@portsmouthcc.gov.uk with any further queries.

env_tokens.protocol_mayor:
The following notes will help you through the protocol which can be involved in a visit by the Lord Mayor, but please do not hesitate to phone the Lord Mayor's Office should you need any further advice on (023) 9283 4706.

It will help others involved in the visit if you pass this information on to them.

MEET AND GREET

  • Please ensure that the Lord Mayor (and Lady Mayoress if attending) are met on arrival and escorted throughout the engagement. The person escorting them should also explain precisely what duties the Lord Mayor and/or Lady Mayoress are expected to perform.

FORMS OF ADDRESS

  • In writing: 'The Lord Mayor of Portsmouth, Councillor Jason Fazackarley'
  • In writing, including the Lady Mayoress 'The Lord Mayor of Portsmouth and the Lady Mayoress, Councillor Jason Fazackarley and Miss Helen Mitchell'
  • In speech: either 'My Lord Mayor' (usually at the beginning of a formal speech) or 'Lord Mayor' (in everyday speech)
  • In speech for the Lady Mayoress 'Lady Mayoress'
  • On place cards: 'The Lord Mayor of Portsmouth' 'The Lady Mayoress of Portsmouth'
  • For inclusion on a plaque etc 'The Right Worshipful the Lord Mayor of Portsmouth'

SEATING PLANS

  • The Lord Mayor should be seated on the immediate right hand side of the host (ess), with the Lady Mayoress on the host (ess) immediate left.
  • If there are other distinguished guests attending, please consult the Lord Mayor's Office as to the most appropriate seating arrangement.

env_tokens.DD_Guarantee:
The Direct Debit Guarantee


• This Guarantee is offered by all Banks and Building Societies that accept instructions to pay Direct Debits.


• If there are any changes to the amount, date or frequency of your Direct Debit we will notify you 10 working days in advance of your account being debited or as otherwise agreed. If you request us to collect a payment, confirmation of the amount and date will be given to you at the time of the request.


• If an error is made in the payment of your Direct Debit by us or your bank or building society, you are entitled to a full and immediate refund of the amount paid from your bank or building society.


• If you receive a refund you are not entitled to, you must pay it back when we ask you to.


• You can cancel a Direct Debit at any time by simply contacting your bank or building society. Written confirmation may be required. Please also notify us.

env_tokens.RRM_emailBody_redHanger:
The contractor has fed back to us that your recycling bin was contaminated, this means that there is something in the bin that we do not take. To clarify, we only take the following items as recycling:

  • Paper (not shredded)
  • Card
  • Plastic bottles (no other plastics)
  • Cans
  • Tins
  • Aerosols

    • Please check your bin and remove any items not listed above; because it was reported as contaminated, the contractor will not be returning to collect it so you will need to keep it until the next collection. If you have surplus recycling next time, you can put it in a cardboard box beside your recycling bin.
env_tokens.ExitInterview:
Exit Interviews

Employees who are leaving PCC voluntarily are entitled to an exit interview. Please follow the link to the Exit Interview policy.

Please read the exit interview policy and undertake the exit interview as appropriate. This can be a fairly informal process and should be recorded on the exit interview form. At the start of the exit interview, you should explain the purpose of the interview and explain that this will not affect their work record or any future references.

You also need to explain that a record of the interview will be kept and also shared with appropriate parties. You can agree that this does not happen until after they have left. Completed forms need to be returned to HR as soon as it is completed or within one week of the individual leaving.

If the individual declines an exit interview, or if for operational reasons you are unable to undertake an exit interview with the individual, then please encourage them to complete the Exit Questionnaire on EBS under HR Employee Self-Service.
env_tokens.UniversalCreditStatment:
You can no longer make a new claim for Housing Benefit (HB) in this area unless you are in one of the groups that can still make a claim. If you cannot claim Housing Benefit you will need to claim Universal Credit (UC).

If you can answer yes to any of the following questions please continue with your claim for housing benefit

  • Do you live in supported accommodation and receive care support or supervision from your landlord or by an organisation on your landlord’s behalf?
  • Have you been placed in temporary accommodation by the Housing Team as part of their duties under homelessness legislation?
  • Are you a single claimant that has reached state pension age?
  • Are you part of a couple who have both reached state pension age?
  • Are you a member of a couple where one member has reached state pension age and
    • One member is under 18, or
    • One member is absent from home due to being in prison for 6 months or longer, or
    • One member is not eligible to claim benefits in the UK, or
    • One of you is absent from Great Britain for more than one month

If you claim UC your rent will be paid by the Department for Work and Pensions (DWP) but you will need to make a claim for Council Tax Reduction (CTR) with us, please continue with your claim for council tax support only.

The council may also be required to disclose personal information to third parties (such as the police, Department for Work and Pensions or Cabinet Office for the National Fraud Initiative) for the purposes of preventing or detecting crime or apprehending or prosecuting offenders.

The details you provide will be held on a database by Euclid Limited who are contracted by Portsmouth City Council to operate the Card Management System for Concessionary Fares. Euclid Limited will only use this information for the purpose of administering your Travel Pass.

Portsmouth City Council uses an online form service supplied by Granicus.

Granicus does not use your personal information for anything other than providing the service to the Council and will not pass on your information for promotional purposes to any other person or organisation (third parties).

For more information about how Granicus uses personal information, view the Granicus govService Subscriber Privacy Policy

For more information about how Portsmouth City Council uses personal information, view the Council's Data Protection Privacy Notice
env_tokens.ElectricVehicleSubmission:
Thank you for your interest in the On-Street Residential Charge Point Scheme (ORCS) for Portsmouth. Your {form_name} form has been received.

The reference number is {case_ref}.

We shall review your request and study the technical feasibility of the proposed location. If affirmative, we will then add it to the bid that we will submit to the Office for Zero Emission Vehicles (OZEV) for funding for the on-street charge points.

Please note that council-installed on-street charging points/bays are for the use of any electric vehicle driver and not just the original requestor of the facility.

You can find details of all existing charge points in Portsmouth via: https://travel.portsmouth.gov.uk/electric-vehicle-charge-point-locations/

The installation date is yet to be determined, but you can access the following link for updates about the scheme - EV charging

We hope this information is helpful. We have an online Frequently Asked Questions list but do contact us at transportplan@portsmouthcc.gov.uk with any further queries.

env_tokens.mayoralAssdTable:
20 + Compelling - significant signs that engagement is worthy of representation by the Civic Head
10 - 20 Marginal - Civic head to consider and provide a summary of the merits of representation in consultation with the Civic Officer.
Below 10 No apparent benefit - attendance must be justified by the Civic Head.
env_tokens.protocol_mayor:
The following notes will help you through the protocol which can be involved in a visit by the Lord Mayor, but please do not hesitate to phone the Lord Mayor's Office should you need any further advice on (023) 9283 4706.

It will help others involved in the visit if you pass this information on to them.

MEET AND GREET

  • Please ensure that the Lord Mayor (and Lady Mayoress if attending) are met on arrival and escorted throughout the engagement. The person escorting them should also explain precisely what duties the Lord Mayor and/or Lady Mayoress are expected to perform.

FORMS OF ADDRESS

  • In writing: 'The Lord Mayor of Portsmouth, Councillor Jason Fazackarley'
  • In writing, including the Lady Mayoress 'The Lord Mayor of Portsmouth and the Lady Mayoress, Councillor Jason Fazackarley and Miss Helen Mitchell'
  • In speech: either 'My Lord Mayor' (usually at the beginning of a formal speech) or 'Lord Mayor' (in everyday speech)
  • In speech for the Lady Mayoress 'Lady Mayoress'
  • On place cards: 'The Lord Mayor of Portsmouth' 'The Lady Mayoress of Portsmouth'
  • For inclusion on a plaque etc 'The Right Worshipful the Lord Mayor of Portsmouth'

SEATING PLANS

  • The Lord Mayor should be seated on the immediate right hand side of the host (ess), with the Lady Mayoress on the host (ess) immediate left.
  • If there are other distinguished guests attending, please consult the Lord Mayor's Office as to the most appropriate seating arrangement.

env_tokens.DD_Guarantee:
The Direct Debit Guarantee


• This Guarantee is offered by all Banks and Building Societies that accept instructions to pay Direct Debits.


• If there are any changes to the amount, date or frequency of your Direct Debit we will notify you 10 working days in advance of your account being debited or as otherwise agreed. If you request us to collect a payment, confirmation of the amount and date will be given to you at the time of the request.


• If an error is made in the payment of your Direct Debit by us or your bank or building society, you are entitled to a full and immediate refund of the amount paid from your bank or building society.


• If you receive a refund you are not entitled to, you must pay it back when we ask you to.


• You can cancel a Direct Debit at any time by simply contacting your bank or building society. Written confirmation may be required. Please also notify us.

env_tokens.DP_Para_RBE:
Privacy notice

Portsmouth City Council will process your personal information appropriately and legally in accordance with the relevant national legislation. The details you provide will be held in a secure system or database and will only be shared with other organisations where the law allows.

We may be required by law to disclose your personal information without your consent for the purposes of protecting the council's legitimate interests (for example to recover debts that are owed to Portsmouth City Council), to fulfil a legal obligation (such as bringing empty properties back into use) or for the purposes of preventing or detecting crime/fraud or apprehending and prosecuting offenders (for example to the Police, the Cabinet Office or Department for Work and Pension or as part of the National Fraud Initiative) or where we have a statutory duty to do so. For more information visit the National Fraud Initiative.

env_tokens.RRM_emailBody_redHanger:
The contractor has fed back to us that your recycling bin was contaminated, this means that there is something in the bin that we do not take. To clarify, we only take the following items as recycling:

  • Paper (not shredded)
  • Card
  • Plastic bottles (no other plastics)
  • Cans
  • Tins
  • Aerosols

    • Please check your bin and remove any items not listed above; because it was reported as contaminated, the contractor will not be returning to collect it so you will need to keep it until the next collection. If you have surplus recycling next time, you can put it in a cardboard box beside your recycling bin.
env_tokens.ExitInterview:
Exit Interviews

Employees who are leaving PCC voluntarily are entitled to an exit interview. Please follow the link to the Exit Interview policy.

Please read the exit interview policy and undertake the exit interview as appropriate. This can be a fairly informal process and should be recorded on the exit interview form. At the start of the exit interview, you should explain the purpose of the interview and explain that this will not affect their work record or any future references.

You also need to explain that a record of the interview will be kept and also shared with appropriate parties. You can agree that this does not happen until after they have left. Completed forms need to be returned to HR as soon as it is completed or within one week of the individual leaving.

If the individual declines an exit interview, or if for operational reasons you are unable to undertake an exit interview with the individual, then please encourage them to complete the Exit Questionnaire on EBS under HR Employee Self-Service.
env_tokens.UniversalCreditStatment:
You can no longer make a new claim for Housing Benefit (HB) in this area unless you are in one of the groups that can still make a claim. If you cannot claim Housing Benefit you will need to claim Universal Credit (UC).

If you can answer yes to any of the following questions please continue with your claim for housing benefit

  • Do you live in supported accommodation and receive care support or supervision from your landlord or by an organisation on your landlord’s behalf?
  • Have you been placed in temporary accommodation by the Housing Team as part of their duties under homelessness legislation?
  • Are you a single claimant that has reached state pension age?
  • Are you part of a couple who have both reached state pension age?
  • Are you a member of a couple where one member has reached state pension age and
    • One member is under 18, or
    • One member is absent from home due to being in prison for 6 months or longer, or
    • One member is not eligible to claim benefits in the UK, or
    • One of you is absent from Great Britain for more than one month

If you claim UC your rent will be paid by the Department for Work and Pensions (DWP) but you will need to make a claim for Council Tax Reduction (CTR) with us, please continue with your claim for council tax support only.

env_tokens.ElectricVehicleSubmission:
Thank you for your interest in the On-Street Residential Charge Point Scheme (ORCS) for Portsmouth. Your {form_name} form has been received.

The reference number is {case_ref}.

We shall review your request and study the technical feasibility of the proposed location. If affirmative, we will then add it to the bid that we will submit to the Office for Zero Emission Vehicles (OZEV) for funding for the on-street charge points.

Please note that council-installed on-street charging points/bays are for the use of any electric vehicle driver and not just the original requestor of the facility.

You can find details of all existing charge points in Portsmouth via: https://travel.portsmouth.gov.uk/electric-vehicle-charge-point-locations/

The installation date is yet to be determined, but you can access the following link for updates about the scheme - EV charging

We hope this information is helpful. We have an online Frequently Asked Questions list but do contact us at transportplan@portsmouthcc.gov.uk with any further queries.

10 - 20 Marginal - Civic head to consider and provide a summary of the merits of representation in consultation with the Civic Officer.
Below 10 No apparent benefit - attendance must be justified by the Civic Head.
env_tokens.protocol_mayor:
The following notes will help you through the protocol which can be involved in a visit by the Lord Mayor, but please do not hesitate to phone the Lord Mayor's Office should you need any further advice on (023) 9283 4706.

It will help others involved in the visit if you pass this information on to them.

MEET AND GREET

  • Please ensure that the Lord Mayor (and Lady Mayoress if attending) are met on arrival and escorted throughout the engagement. The person escorting them should also explain precisely what duties the Lord Mayor and/or Lady Mayoress are expected to perform.

FORMS OF ADDRESS

  • In writing: 'The Lord Mayor of Portsmouth, Councillor Jason Fazackarley'
  • In writing, including the Lady Mayoress 'The Lord Mayor of Portsmouth and the Lady Mayoress, Councillor Jason Fazackarley and Miss Helen Mitchell'
  • In speech: either 'My Lord Mayor' (usually at the beginning of a formal speech) or 'Lord Mayor' (in everyday speech)
  • In speech for the Lady Mayoress 'Lady Mayoress'
  • On place cards: 'The Lord Mayor of Portsmouth' 'The Lady Mayoress of Portsmouth'
  • For inclusion on a plaque etc 'The Right Worshipful the Lord Mayor of Portsmouth'

SEATING PLANS

  • The Lord Mayor should be seated on the immediate right hand side of the host (ess), with the Lady Mayoress on the host (ess) immediate left.
  • If there are other distinguished guests attending, please consult the Lord Mayor's Office as to the most appropriate seating arrangement.

env_tokens.DD_Guarantee:
The Direct Debit Guarantee


• This Guarantee is offered by all Banks and Building Societies that accept instructions to pay Direct Debits.


• If there are any changes to the amount, date or frequency of your Direct Debit we will notify you 10 working days in advance of your account being debited or as otherwise agreed. If you request us to collect a payment, confirmation of the amount and date will be given to you at the time of the request.


• If an error is made in the payment of your Direct Debit by us or your bank or building society, you are entitled to a full and immediate refund of the amount paid from your bank or building society.


• If you receive a refund you are not entitled to, you must pay it back when we ask you to.


• You can cancel a Direct Debit at any time by simply contacting your bank or building society. Written confirmation may be required. Please also notify us.

env_tokens.RRM_emailBody_redHanger:
The contractor has fed back to us that your recycling bin was contaminated, this means that there is something in the bin that we do not take. To clarify, we only take the following items as recycling:

  • Paper (not shredded)
  • Card
  • Plastic bottles (no other plastics)
  • Cans
  • Tins
  • Aerosols

    • Please check your bin and remove any items not listed above; because it was reported as contaminated, the contractor will not be returning to collect it so you will need to keep it until the next collection. If you have surplus recycling next time, you can put it in a cardboard box beside your recycling bin.
env_tokens.ExitInterview:
Exit Interviews

Employees who are leaving PCC voluntarily are entitled to an exit interview. Please follow the link to the Exit Interview policy.

Please read the exit interview policy and undertake the exit interview as appropriate. This can be a fairly informal process and should be recorded on the exit interview form. At the start of the exit interview, you should explain the purpose of the interview and explain that this will not affect their work record or any future references.

You also need to explain that a record of the interview will be kept and also shared with appropriate parties. You can agree that this does not happen until after they have left. Completed forms need to be returned to HR as soon as it is completed or within one week of the individual leaving.

If the individual declines an exit interview, or if for operational reasons you are unable to undertake an exit interview with the individual, then please encourage them to complete the Exit Questionnaire on EBS under HR Employee Self-Service.
env_tokens.UniversalCreditStatment:
You can no longer make a new claim for Housing Benefit (HB) in this area unless you are in one of the groups that can still make a claim. If you cannot claim Housing Benefit you will need to claim Universal Credit (UC).

If you can answer yes to any of the following questions please continue with your claim for housing benefit

  • Do you live in supported accommodation and receive care support or supervision from your landlord or by an organisation on your landlord’s behalf?
  • Have you been placed in temporary accommodation by the Housing Team as part of their duties under homelessness legislation?
  • Are you a single claimant that has reached state pension age?
  • Are you part of a couple who have both reached state pension age?
  • Are you a member of a couple where one member has reached state pension age and
    • One member is under 18, or
    • One member is absent from home due to being in prison for 6 months or longer, or
    • One member is not eligible to claim benefits in the UK, or
    • One of you is absent from Great Britain for more than one month

If you claim UC your rent will be paid by the Department for Work and Pensions (DWP) but you will need to make a claim for Council Tax Reduction (CTR) with us, please continue with your claim for council tax support only.

Thank you for your interest in the On-Street Residential Charge Point Scheme (ORCS) for Portsmouth. Your {form_name} form has been received.

We shall review your request and study the technical feasibility of the proposed location. If affirmative, we will then add it to the bid that we will submit to the Office for Zero Emission Vehicles (OZEV) for funding for the on-street charge points.

Please note that council-installed on-street charging points/bays are for the use of any electric vehicle driver and not just the original requestor of the facility.

You can find details of all existing charge points in Portsmouth via: https://travel.portsmouth.gov.uk/electric-vehicle-charge-point-locations/

The installation date is yet to be determined, but you can access the following link for updates about the scheme - EV charging

env_tokens.mayoralAssdTable:
20 + Compelling - significant signs that engagement is worthy of representation by the Civic Head
10 - 20 Marginal - Civic head to consider and provide a summary of the merits of representation in consultation with the Civic Officer.
Below 10 No apparent benefit - attendance must be justified by the Civic Head.
env_tokens.protocol_mayor:
The following notes will help you through the protocol which can be involved in a visit by the Lord Mayor, but please do not hesitate to phone the Lord Mayor's Office should you need any further advice on (023) 9283 4706.

It will help others involved in the visit if you pass this information on to them.

MEET AND GREET

  • Please ensure that the Lord Mayor (and Lady Mayoress if attending) are met on arrival and escorted throughout the engagement. The person escorting them should also explain precisely what duties the Lord Mayor and/or Lady Mayoress are expected to perform.

FORMS OF ADDRESS

  • In writing: 'The Lord Mayor of Portsmouth, Councillor Jason Fazackarley'
  • In writing, including the Lady Mayoress 'The Lord Mayor of Portsmouth and the Lady Mayoress, Councillor Jason Fazackarley and Miss Helen Mitchell'
  • In speech: either 'My Lord Mayor' (usually at the beginning of a formal speech) or 'Lord Mayor' (in everyday speech)
  • In speech for the Lady Mayoress 'Lady Mayoress'
  • On place cards: 'The Lord Mayor of Portsmouth' 'The Lady Mayoress of Portsmouth'
  • For inclusion on a plaque etc 'The Right Worshipful the Lord Mayor of Portsmouth'

SEATING PLANS

  • The Lord Mayor should be seated on the immediate right hand side of the host (ess), with the Lady Mayoress on the host (ess) immediate left.
  • If there are other distinguished guests attending, please consult the Lord Mayor's Office as to the most appropriate seating arrangement.

env_tokens.DD_Guarantee:
The Direct Debit Guarantee


• This Guarantee is offered by all Banks and Building Societies that accept instructions to pay Direct Debits.


• If there are any changes to the amount, date or frequency of your Direct Debit we will notify you 10 working days in advance of your account being debited or as otherwise agreed. If you request us to collect a payment, confirmation of the amount and date will be given to you at the time of the request.


• If an error is made in the payment of your Direct Debit by us or your bank or building society, you are entitled to a full and immediate refund of the amount paid from your bank or building society.


• If you receive a refund you are not entitled to, you must pay it back when we ask you to.


• You can cancel a Direct Debit at any time by simply contacting your bank or building society. Written confirmation may be required. Please also notify us.

You can no longer make a new claim for Housing Benefit (HB) in this area unless you are in one of the groups that can still make a claim. If you cannot claim Housing Benefit you will need to claim Universal Credit (UC).

If you can answer yes to any of the following questions please continue with your claim for housing benefit

  • Do you live in supported accommodation and receive care support or supervision from your landlord or by an organisation on your landlord’s behalf?
  • Have you been placed in temporary accommodation by the Housing Team as part of their duties under homelessness legislation?
  • Are you a single claimant that has reached state pension age?
  • Are you part of a couple who have both reached state pension age?
  • Are you a member of a couple where one member has reached state pension age and
    • One member is under 18, or
    • One member is absent from home due to being in prison for 6 months or longer, or
    • One member is not eligible to claim benefits in the UK, or
    • One of you is absent from Great Britain for more than one month

If you claim UC your rent will be paid by the Department for Work and Pensions (DWP) but you will need to make a claim for Council Tax Reduction (CTR) with us, please continue with your claim for council tax support only.

env_tokens.ElectricVehicleSubmission:
Thank you for your interest in the On-Street Residential Charge Point Scheme (ORCS) for Portsmouth. Your {form_name} form has been received.

The reference number is {case_ref}.

We shall review your request and study the technical feasibility of the proposed location. If affirmative, we will then add it to the bid that we will submit to the Office for Zero Emission Vehicles (OZEV) for funding for the on-street charge points.

Please note that council-installed on-street charging points/bays are for the use of any electric vehicle driver and not just the original requestor of the facility.

You can find details of all existing charge points in Portsmouth via: https://travel.portsmouth.gov.uk/electric-vehicle-charge-point-locations/

The installation date is yet to be determined, but you can access the following link for updates about the scheme - EV charging

We hope this information is helpful. We have an online Frequently Asked Questions list but do contact us at transportplan@portsmouthcc.gov.uk with any further queries.

env_tokens.mayoralAssdTable:
20 + Compelling - significant signs that engagement is worthy of representation by the Civic Head
10 - 20 Marginal - Civic head to consider and provide a summary of the merits of representation in consultation with the Civic Officer.
Below 10 No apparent benefit - attendance must be justified by the Civic Head.
env_tokens.protocol_mayor:
The following notes will help you through the protocol which can be involved in a visit by the Lord Mayor, but please do not hesitate to phone the Lord Mayor's Office should you need any further advice on (023) 9283 4706.

It will help others involved in the visit if you pass this information on to them.

MEET AND GREET

  • Please ensure that the Lord Mayor (and Lady Mayoress if attending) are met on arrival and escorted throughout the engagement. The person escorting them should also explain precisely what duties the Lord Mayor and/or Lady Mayoress are expected to perform.

FORMS OF ADDRESS

  • In writing: 'The Lord Mayor of Portsmouth, Councillor Jason Fazackarley'
  • In writing, including the Lady Mayoress 'The Lord Mayor of Portsmouth and the Lady Mayoress, Councillor Jason Fazackarley and Miss Helen Mitchell'
  • In speech: either 'My Lord Mayor' (usually at the beginning of a formal speech) or 'Lord Mayor' (in everyday speech)
  • In speech for the Lady Mayoress 'Lady Mayoress'
  • On place cards: 'The Lord Mayor of Portsmouth' 'The Lady Mayoress of Portsmouth'
  • For inclusion on a plaque etc 'The Right Worshipful the Lord Mayor of Portsmouth'

SEATING PLANS

  • The Lord Mayor should be seated on the immediate right hand side of the host (ess), with the Lady Mayoress on the host (ess) immediate left.
  • If there are other distinguished guests attending, please consult the Lord Mayor's Office as to the most appropriate seating arrangement.
